log.code:
import numpy as np

N = 40
JAIL = 10
G2J = 30
CC = {2, 17, 33}
CH = {7, 22, 36}

# Dice distribution for two 4-sided dice
from collections import Counter
rolls = Counter()
for a in range(1, 5):
    for b in range(1, 5):
        rolls[a+b] += 1
roll_probs = {s: c / 16.0 for s, c in rolls.items()}

cc_moves = {0: 1/16, JAIL: 1/16}  # GO, JAIL
ch_moves = {
    0: 1/16, JAIL: 1/16, 11: 1/16, 24: 1/16, 39: 1/16, 5: 1/16,
}
# next R twice
# next U
# back 3

def next_r(pos):
    for r in (5, 15, 25, 35):
        if r > pos:
            return r
    return 5

def next_u(pos):
    return 12 if pos < 12 or pos >= 28 else 28

# Build transition matrix P[i][j] = prob end at j after one turn starting from i
P = np.zeros((N, N), dtype=float)

for start in range(N):
    # state includes doubles count and jail? For this problem, if in jail and roll doubles? Actually ignore get-out-of-jail rule,
    # assume pay next turn, so no special jail mechanics beyond landing there. Thus no need to track doubles across turns.
    for s, p_roll in roll_probs.items():
        pos = (start + s) % N
        # resolve G2J
        if pos == G2J:
            P[start, JAIL] += p_roll
            continue
        # resolve CC/CH with movement cards only; non-movement cards leave you there.
        if pos in CC:
            # 14/16 stay
            P[start, pos] += p_roll * 14/16
            # GO, JAIL
            P[start, 0] += p_roll * (1/16)
            P[start, JAIL] += p_roll * (1/16)
            continue
        if pos in CH:
            # 6 cards that move to fixed squares
            fixed = [0, JAIL, 11, 24, 39, 5]
            for q in fixed:
                P[start, q] += p_roll * (1/16)
            # next R twice
            P[start, next_r(pos)] += p_roll * (2/16)
            # next U
            P[start, next_u(pos)] += p_roll * (1/16)
            # back 3, then resolve if lands on CC/G2J
            back = (pos - 3) % N
            if back == G2J:
                P[start, JAIL] += p_roll * (1/16)
            elif back in CC:
                P[start, back] += p_roll * (14/16)  # stay on CC
                P[start, 0] += p_roll * (1/16)
                P[start, JAIL] += p_roll * (1/16)
            elif back in CH:
                # back 3 from CH can land on CH? not possible for standard board, but handle recursively if needed
                fixed2 = [0, JAIL, 11, 24, 39, 5]
                for q in fixed2:
                    P[start, q] += p_roll * (1/16) * (1/16)
                P[start, next_r(back)] += p_roll * (1/16) * (2/16)
                P[start, next_u(back)] += p_roll * (1/16) * (1/16)
                bb = (back - 3) % N
                if bb == G2J:
                    P[start, JAIL] += p_roll * (1/16) * (1/16)
                else:
                    P[start, bb] += p_roll * (1/16) * (1/16)
            else:
                P[start, back] += p_roll * (1/16)
            continue
        P[start, pos] += p_roll

# Power iteration for stationary distribution
v = np.ones(N) / N
for _ in range(10000):
    nv = v @ P
    if np.max(np.abs(nv - v)) < 1e-15:
        v = nv
        break
    v = nv

order = sorted(range(N), key=lambda i: (-v[i], i))
print(''.join(f'{i:02d}' for i in order[:3]))
